text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
One day, while I'm strolling around<n>the shopping
district...

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Great. What a mess I've gotten<n>myself into...
Let's see... There's<n>shrimp, mixed tempura,
squid, bell<n>peppers...

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Oh, it's nothing serious. One of the<n>older
shopkeepers in the shopping<n>district gave me a
hefty amount of<n>tempura.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
And while I'm incredibly happy and<n>grateful for
his gift, there's simply no<n>way I can finish it all.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Deep-fried foods are so oily and<n>heavy, and
eating so much in one<n>sitting is guaranteed to
end in a nasty<n>bout of indigestion!
choice_data_list:
Eat it with grated daikon radish to make things
easier!
You should hold a tempura party at the dorm!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text: Grated daikon radish? How would<n>that help?

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Interesting... That sounds just like the<n>kind of
thing a grandmother would<n>say, and I love golden
nuggets of<n>wisdom like that.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Speaking of which, I remember my<n>aunt teaching
me a good way to use<n>up leftover tempura a long
time ago.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Okay, time to buy some daikon<n>radishes at the
supermarket! I'll be<n>able to eat as much tempura
as I want<n>with their help!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
At the dorm? Oh, that's a great idea. I<n>can just
leave it in the cafeteria!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
That way, I don't have to go around<n>telling
everyone about the tempura,<n>and anyone who
wants some can<n>help themselves!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
It'll be great exposure for the tasty<n>tempura
place in the shopping district<n>too. Yup, all's well
that ends well.
